title: lithocarpus indutus
intro: Lithocarpus indutus, also known as the Bur Oak or Valley Oak, is a type of evergreen tree native to the mountainous regions of China. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to have various health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and protecting against infections. The leaves of the Lithocarpus indutus tree can be used to make herbal preparations like infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues including digestive problems and respiratory infections. In addition to its medicinal properties, the tree is also valued for its wood, which is durable and resistant to decay, making it a popular choice for furniture and construction.
